item.title: 编写弹性组件 | Writing Resilient Components
item.description:
当人们开始学习 React 时，他们通常会要求一份风格指南。 虽然在项目中应用一些一致的规则是个好主意，但其中很多都是任意的——因此 React 对它们没有强烈的意见。

您可以使用不同类型的系统，更喜欢函数声明或箭头函数，按字母顺序或您喜欢的顺序对您的道具进行排序。

这种灵活性允许将 React 集成到具有现有约定的项目中。 但它也引发了无休止的争论。

但我不认为风格指南很好地抓住了这些原则。 不要被想象中的问题分心

在我们谈论组件设计原则之前，我想说几句关于风格指南的事。 这不是一个流行的观点，但需要有人说出来！

在 JavaScript 社区中，有一些由 linter 强制执行的严格的自以为是的风格指南。 我个人的观察是，它们往往会产生比其价值更多的摩擦。 我数不清有多少次有人向我展示了一些绝对有效的代码并说“React 抱怨这个”，但这是他们的 lint 配置抱怨！ 这会导致三个问题：

  • 人们习惯于将 linter 视为一个过分热心的嘈杂守门人，而不是一个有用的工具。 有用的警告被样式 nits 的海洋淹没了。 因此，人们在调试时不会扫描 linter 消息，而会错过有用的提示。 此外，不太习惯编写 JavaScript 的人（例如，设计师）使用代码会更加困难。

  • 人们不会学会区分某种模式的有效和无效使用。 例如，有一条流行的规则禁止在componentDidMount中调用setState 。 但如果它总是“坏”，React 根本不会允许它！ 它有一个合法的用例，那就是测量 DOM 节点布局——例如定位工具提示。 我见过人们通过添加一个完全没有抓住要点的setTimeout来“解决”这个规则。

  • 最终，人们会采用“执行者心态”，并对不会带来有意义的差异但很容易在代码中扫描的事物产生自以为是的态度。 每当我对执行这样的规则有强烈的感觉时，更深入地观察就会发现我在这条规则上投入了情感上的努力——并且很难让它消失。 它让我在没有改进我的代码的情况下产生一种虚假的成就感。

我是说我们应该停止 linting 吗？ 一点也不！

通过良好的配置，linter 是一个很好的工具，可以在错误发生之前捕获它们。 它过于专注于风格，这会让人分心。

你的 Lint 配置

这是我建议你在星期一做的事情。 召集你的团队半小时，检查项目配置中启用的每条 lint 规则，然后问问自己： “这条规则是否曾帮助我们发现错误？” 如果没有，请将其关闭。 至少，您的团队应该有一个流程来删除导致摩擦的规则。 不要假设你或其他人在一年前添加到你的 lint 配置中的任何东西都是“最佳实践”。 质疑并寻找答案。 不要让任何人告诉您您不够聪明，无法选择 lint 规则。

但是格式化呢？ 使用Prettier并忘掉“style nits”。 如果另一个工具可以为您解决问题，您不需要工具来对您大喊大叫以放置额外的空间。 使用 linter 查找bug ，而不是强制美观。

当然，编码风格的某些方面与格式没有直接关系，但在整个项目中不一致时仍然很烦人。

然而，它们中的许多都太微妙了，无论如何都无法用 lint 规则捕捉到。 这就是为什么在团队成员之间建立信任并以 wiki 页面或简短设计指南的形式分享有用的知识很重要。

并非一切都值得自动化！ 从实际阅读此类指南中的基本原理中获得的见解可能比遵循“规则”更有价值。

但是，如果遵循严格的风格指南会让人分心，那么什么才是真正重要的呢？

这就是这篇文章的主题。

没有多少缩进或按字母顺序对导入进行排序可以修复损坏的设计。 因此，我不会关注某些代码的外观，而是关注它的工作原理。 我发现一些组件设计原则非常有用：

  1. 不要停止数据流
  2. 随时准备渲染
  3. 没有组件是单例
  4. 保持本地状态隔离

即使您不使用 React，对于具有单向数据流的任何 UI 组件模型，您也可能会通过反复试验发现相同的原则。

原则 1：不要停止数据流

不要停止渲染中的数据流

当有人使用你的组件时，他们希望他们可以随着时间的推移向它传递不同的道具，并且该组件将反映这些变化：

 // isOk might be driven by state and can change at any time < Button color = { isOk ? ' blue ' : ' red ' } />

一般来说，这就是 React 默认的工作方式。 如果您在Button组件中使用color属性，您将看到上面为该渲染提供的值：

 function Button ({ color , children }) { return ( // ✅ `color` is always fresh! < button className = { ' Button- ' + color } > { children } </ button > ); }

然而，学习 React 的一个常见错误是将 props 复制到 state 中：

 class Button extends React . Component { state = { color : this . props . color }; render () { const { color } = this . state ; // 🔴 `color` is stale! return ( < button className = { ' Button- ' + color } > { this . props . children } </ button > ); } }

如果您在 React 之外使用类，这乍一看似乎更直观。 但是，通过将道具复制到状态中，您将忽略对其的所有更新。

 // 🔴 No longer works for updates with the above implementation < Button color = { isOk ? ' blue ' : ' red ' } />

在这种行为是故意的极少数情况下，请确保调用该道具initialColor或defaultColor以澄清对其的更改被忽略。

但通常你会希望直接在组件中读取 props并避免将 props（或从 props 计算的任何东西）复制到状态中：

 function Button ({ color , children }) { return ( // ✅ `color` is always fresh! < button className = { ' Button- ' + color } > { children } </ button > ); }

计算值是人们有时试图将 props 复制到状态的另一个原因。 例如，假设我们基于以背景color作为参数的昂贵计算来确定按钮文本颜色：

 class Button extends React . Component { state = { textColor : slowlyCalculateTextColor ( this . props . color ) }; render () { return ( < button className = { ' Button- ' + this . props . color + ' Button-text- ' + this . state . textColor // 🔴 Stale on `color` prop updates } > { this . props . children } </ button > ); } }

这个组件有问题，因为它不会在color属性更改时重新计算this.state.textColor 。 最简单的解决方法是将textColor计算移动到render方法中，并将其PureComponent ：

 class Button extends React . PureComponent { render () { const textColor = slowlyCalculateTextColor ( this . props . color ); return ( < button className = { ' Button- ' + this . props . color + ' Button-text- ' + textColor // ✅ Always fresh } > { this . props . children } </ button > ); } }

问题解决了！ 现在，如果 props 发生变化，我们将重新计算textColor ，但我们避免了对相同 props 的昂贵计算。

但是，我们可能希望进一步优化它。 如果是children属性改变了怎么办？ 在这种情况下重新计算textColor似乎很不幸。 我们的第二次尝试可能是调用componentDidUpdate中的计算：

 class Button extends React . Component { state = { textColor : slowlyCalculateTextColor ( this . props . color ) }; componentDidUpdate ( prevProps ) { if ( prevProps . color !== this . props . color ) { // 😔 Extra re-render for every update this . setState ({ textColor : slowlyCalculateTextColor ( this . props . color ), }); } } render () { return ( < button className = { ' Button- ' + this . props . color + ' Button-text- ' + this . state . textColor // ✅ Fresh on final render } > { this . props . children } </ button > ); } }

然而，这意味着我们的组件在每次更改后都会进行第二次重新渲染。 如果我们试图优化它，那也不是理想的。

您可以为此使用遗留的componentWillReceiveProps生命周期。 然而，人们也经常把副作用放在那里。 反过来，这通常会导致即将到来的并发渲染功能出现问题，例如 Time Slicing 和 Suspense 。 而且“更安全”的getDerivedStateFromProps方法很笨重。

让我们退后一步。 实际上，我们想要memoization 。 我们有一些输入，我们不想重新计算输出，除非输入发生变化。

使用类，您可以使用帮助程序进行记忆。 然而，Hooks 更进一步，为您提供了一种内置的方式来记忆昂贵的计算：

 function Button ({ color , children }) { const textColor = useMemo ( () => slowlyCalculateTextColor ( color ), [ color ] // ✅ Don't recalculate until `color` changes ); return ( < button className = { ' Button- ' + color + ' Button-text- ' + textColor } > { children } </ button > ); }

这就是您需要的所有代码！

在类组件中，您可以为此使用像memoize-one这样的助手。 在函数组件中， useMemo Hook 为您提供了类似的功能。

现在我们看到，即使优化昂贵的计算也不是将 props 复制到状态的好理由。 我们的渲染结果应该尊重道具的变化。

不要在副作用中停止数据流

到目前为止，我们已经讨论了如何使渲染结果与道具变化保持一致。 避免将道具复制到状态是其中的一部分。 然而，重要的是副作用（例如数据获取）也是数据流的一部分。

考虑这个 React 组件：

 class SearchResults extends React . Component { state = { data : null }; componentDidMount () { this . fetchResults (); } fetchResults () { const url = this . getFetchUrl (); // Do the fetching... } getFetchUrl () { return ' http://myapi/results?query ' + this . props . query ; } render () { // ... } }

很多 React 组件都是这样的——但如果我们仔细观察，就会发现一个错误。 但是如果query属性改变了怎么办？ 在我们的组件中，什么都不会发生。 这意味着我们组件的副作用不考虑其道具的变化。 这是 React 应用程序中非常常见的错误来源。

为了修复我们的组件，我们需要：

  • 查看componentDidMount以及从中调用的每个方法。
    • 在我们的示例中，它是fetchResults和getFetchUrl 。
  • 写下这些方法使用的所有道具和状态。
    • 在我们的示例中，这是this.props.query 。
  • 确保每当这些道具发生变化时，我们都会重新运行副作用。
    • 我们可以通过添加componentDidUpdate方法来做到这一点。
 class SearchResults extends React . Component { state = { data : null }; componentDidMount () { this . fetchResults (); } componentDidUpdate ( prevProps ) { if ( prevProps . query !== this . props . query ) { // ✅ Refetch on change this . fetchResults (); } } fetchResults () { const url = this . getFetchUrl (); // Do the fetching... } getFetchUrl () { return ' http://myapi/results?query ' + this . props . query ; // ✅ Updates are handled } render () { // ... } }

现在我们的代码尊重对道具的所有更改，即使是副作用。

但是，记住不要再次破坏它是一项挑战。 例如，我们可以将currentPage添加到本地状态，并在getFetchUrl中使用它：

 class SearchResults extends React . Component { state = { data : null , currentPage : 0 , }; componentDidMount () { this . fetchResults (); } componentDidUpdate ( prevProps ) { if ( prevProps . query !== this . props . query ) { this . fetchResults (); } } fetchResults () { const url = this . getFetchUrl (); // Do the fetching... } getFetchUrl () { return ( ' http://myapi/results?query ' + this . props . query + ' &page= ' + this . state . currentPage // 🔴 Updates are ignored ); } render () { // ... } }

唉，我们的代码又是错误的，因为我们的副作用不考虑对currentPage的更改。

渲染和副作用都应该反映数据流的变化，而不是忽略它们！

要修复我们的代码，我们可以重复上述步骤：

  • 查看componentDidMount以及从中调用的每个方法。
    • 在我们的示例中，它是fetchResults和getFetchUrl 。
  • 写下这些方法使用的所有道具和状态。
    • 在我们的示例中，这是this.props.query和this.state.currentPage 。
  • 确保每当这些道具发生变化时，我们都会重新运行副作用。
    • 我们可以通过更改componentDidUpdate方法来做到这一点。

让我们修复我们的组件以处理对currentPage状态的更新：

 class SearchResults extends React . Component { state = { data : null , currentPage : 0 , }; componentDidMount () { this . fetchResults (); } componentDidUpdate ( prevProps , prevState ) { if ( prevState . currentPage !== this . state . currentPage || // ✅ Refetch on change prevProps . query !== this . props . query ) { this . fetchResults (); } } fetchResults () { const url = this . getFetchUrl (); // Do the fetching... } getFetchUrl () { return ( ' http://myapi/results?query ' + this . props . query + ' &page= ' + this . state . currentPage // ✅ Updates are handled ); } render () { // ... } }

如果我们能够以某种方式自动捕获这些错误，那不是很好吗？ 这不是 linter 可以帮助我们的事情吗？

不幸的是，自动检查类组件的一致性太困难了。 任何方法都可以调用任何其他方法。 静态分析来自componentDidMount和componentDidUpdate的调用充满了误报。

但是，可以设计一个可以静态分析一致性的 API。 我们将逻辑放在效果中，这样可以更容易地看到它依赖于React 数据流中的哪些值。 这些值称为“依赖项”，在我们的示例中它们是[currentPage, query] 。

请注意，这个“效果依赖性”数组并不是一个新概念。 在一个类中，我们必须通过所有方法调用来搜索这些“依赖项”。 这反过来又让我们自动验证它们：

请注意，无论您是将组件编写为类还是函数，都必须尊重所有 prop 和状态更新以获取效果。

使用类 API，您必须自己考虑一致性，并验证对每个相关属性或状态的更改是否由componentDidUpdate处理。 否则，您的组件无法适应 prop 和 state 的变化。 这甚至不是 React 特有的问题。 它适用于任何允许您分别处理“创建”和“更新”的 UI 库。

一开始可能会觉得不熟悉，但结果是您的组件对逻辑的变化变得更有弹性。 由于“依赖关系”现在是明确的，我们可以使用 lint 规则验证效果是否一致。 我们正在使用 linter 来捕捉错误！

不要停止优化中的数据流

还有一种情况，您可能会不小心忽略对道具的更改。 当您手动优化组件时，可能会发生此错误。

请注意，使用浅层相等的优化方法（如PureComponent和React.memo以及默认比较是安全的。

但是，如果您尝试通过编写自己的比较来“优化”组件，您可能会错误地忘记比较函数属性：

 class Button extends React . Component { shouldComponentUpdate ( prevProps ) { // 🔴 Doesn't compare this.props.onClick return this . props . color !== prevProps . color ; } render () { const onClick = this . props . onClick ; // 🔴 Doesn't reflect updates const textColor = slowlyCalculateTextColor ( this . props . color ); return ( < button onClick = { onClick } className = { ' Button- ' + this . props . color + ' Button-text- ' + textColor } > { this . props . children } </ button > ); } }

一开始很容易错过这个错误，因为对于类，你通常会向下传递一个方法，所以它无论如何都会有相同的标识：

 class MyForm extends React . Component { handleClick = () => { // ✅ Always the same function // Do something } render () { return ( <> < h1 > Hello! </ h1 > < Button color = 'green' onClick = { this . handleClick } > Press me </ Button > </> ) } }

所以我们的优化不会立即中断。 但是，如果它随时间变化但其他道具没有变化，它将继续“看到”旧的onClick值：

 class MyForm extends React . Component { state = { isEnabled : true }; handleClick = () => { this . setState ({ isEnabled : false }); // Do something } render () { return ( <> < h1 > Hello! </ h1 > < Button color = 'green' onClick = { // 🔴 Button ignores updates to the onClick prop this . state . isEnabled ? this . handleClick : null } > Press me </ Button > </> ) } }

在这个例子中，点击按钮应该禁用它——但这并没有发生，因为Button组件忽略了对onClick属性的任何更新。

如果函数标识本身依赖于可能随时间变化的东西，这可能会变得更加混乱，例如本例中的draft.content ：

 drafts . map ( draft => < Button color = 'blue' key = { draft . id } onClick = { // 🔴 Button ignores updates to the onClick prop this . handlePublish . bind ( this , draft . content ) } > Publish </ Button > )

虽然draft.content可能会随着时间的推移发生变化，但我们的Button组件忽略了对onClick属性的更改，因此它继续看到onClick绑定方法的“第一个版本”与原始draft.content 。

那么我们如何避免这个问题呢？

我建议避免手动实现shouldComponentUpdate并避免指定与React.memo()的自定义比较。 在类中， PureComponent具有相同的行为。

这确保将不同的函数作为 prop 传递将始终有效。

如果您坚持自定义比较，请确保您没有跳过函数：

 shouldComponentUpdate ( prevProps ) { // ✅ Compares this.props.onClick return ( this . props . color !== prevProps . color || this . props . onClick !== prevProps . onClick ); }

正如我之前提到的，在类组件中很容易遗漏这个问题，因为方法标识通常是稳定的（但并非总是如此——这就是 bug 变得难以调试的地方）。 使用 Hooks，情况有点不同：

总结这一节，不要停止数据流！

每当您使用道具和状态时，请考虑如果它们发生变化会发生什么。 在大多数情况下，组件不应该以不同方式对待初始渲染和更新。 这使得它对逻辑的变化具有弹性。

对于类，在生命周期方法中使用 props 和 state 时很容易忘记更新。 原则 2：随时准备渲染

您描述 UI 在任何给定时刻应该如何显示，而 React 使之成为现实。 利用该模型！

不要试图在您的组件行为中引入不必要的时序假设。 您的组件应该随时准备好重新渲染。

怎么能违背这个原则呢？

在这个例子中，我们将value保存在本地状态中，但我们也从 props 中接收value 。 每当我们“收到新道具”时，我们都会重置状态value 。

这种模式的问题在于它完全依赖于偶然的时机。

也许今天这个组件的父组件很少更新，所以我们的TextInput只在发生重要事情时“接收道具”，比如保存表单。

但是明天您可能会向TextInput的父级添加一些动画。 如果它的父母更频繁地重新渲染，它会不断地“吹走”孩子状态！ 您可以在“您可能不需要派生状态”中阅读有关此问题的更多信息。

那么我们如何解决这个问题呢？

首先，我们需要修复我们的心智模型。 我们需要停止将“接收道具”视为不同于“渲染”的东西。 由父项引起的重新渲染与由我们自己的本地状态更改引起的重新渲染的行为不应该有所不同。 组件应该能够适应更少或更频繁地渲染，否则它们会与其特定的父组件过于耦合。

当你真的想从 props 派生状态时，有几种不同的解决方案，通常你应该使用完全受控的组件：

 // Option 1: Fully controlled component. function TextInput ({ value , onChange }) { return ( < input value = { value } onChange = { onChange } /> ); }

或者您可以使用带有键的不受控组件来重置它：

 // Option 2: Fully uncontrolled component. function TextInput () { const [ value , setValue ] = useState ( '' ); return ( < input value = { value } onChange = { e => setValue ( e . target . value ) } /> ); } // We can reset its internal state later by changing the key: < TextInput key = { formId } />

本节的要点是您的组件不应该仅仅因为它或其父组件更频繁地重新渲染而中断。 如果您避免使用遗留的componentWillReceiveProps生命周期方法，React API 设计会让这一切变得简单。

要对您的组件进行压力测试，您可以暂时将此代码添加到其父级：

 componentDidMount () { // Don't forget to remove this immediately! setInterval (() => this . forceUpdate (), 100 ); }

不要留下这段代码——它只是一种快速检查当父级重新渲染比你预期的更频繁时会发生什么的方法。 它不应该打破孩子！

你可能会想：“我会在 props 改变时继续重置状态，但会用PureComponent防止不必要的重新渲染”。

这段代码应该可以工作，对吧？

 // 🤔 Should prevent unnecessary re-renders... right? class TextInput extends React.PureComponent { state = { value: '' }; // 🔴 Resets local state on every parent render componentWillReceiveProps(nextProps) { this.setState({ value: nextProps.value }); } handleChange = (e) => { this.setState({ value: e.target.value }); }; render() { return ( <input value={this.state.value} onChange={this.handleChange} /> ); } }

乍一看，这个组件似乎解决了在父级重新渲染时“吹走”状态的问题。 毕竟，如果 props 相同，我们就跳过更新——因此componentWillReceiveProps不会被调用。

然而，这给了我们一种虚假的安全感。 该组件仍然无法适应实际的 prop 更改。 例如，如果我们添加另一个经常变化的道具，比如动画style ，我们仍然会“丢失”内部状态：

 < TextInput style = { { opacity : someValueFromState } } value = { // 🔴 componentWillReceiveProps in TextInput // resets to this value on every animation tick. value } />

所以这个方法还是有缺陷的。 我们可以看到PureComponent 、 shouldComponentUpdate和React.memo等各种优化不应该用于控制行为。 仅在有帮助的情况下使用它们来提高性能。 如果删除优化破坏了一个组件，那么它就太脆弱了。

这里的解决方案与我们之前描述的相同。 不要把“领取道具”当作一件特殊的事情。 避免“同步”道具和状态。 在大多数情况下，每个值都应该完全控制（通过 props），或者完全不受控制（在本地状态中）。 尽可能避免派生状态。 随时准备渲染！

原则 3：没有组件是单例的

有时我们假设某个组件​​只显示一次。 比如导航栏。 这可能会持续一段时间。 然而，这种假设通常会导致设计问题，这些问题只会在很久以后才浮出水面。

例如，您可能需要在路由更改时在两个Page组件之间实现动画 — 前一个Page和下一个Page 。 它们都需要在动画期间安装。 但是，您可能会发现这些组件中的每一个都假定它是屏幕上的唯一Page 。

检查这些问题很容易。 只是为了好玩，尝试渲染你的应用程序两次：

 ReactDOM . render ( <> < MyApp /> < MyApp /> </>, document . getElementById ( ' root ' ) );

点击周围。 您的应用程序是否仍按预期运行？ 或者您是否看到奇怪的崩溃和错误？ 偶尔对复杂组件进行这种压力测试是个好主意，并确保它们的多个副本不会相互冲突。

我自己写过几次的问题模式示例是在componentWillUnmount中执行全局状态“清理”：

 componentWillUnmount() { // Resets something in Redux store this.props.resetForm(); }

当然，如果页面上有两个这样的组件，卸载其中一个可能会破坏另一个。 在挂载上重置“全局”状态也好不到哪里去：

 componentDidMount () { // Resets something in Redux store this . props . resetForm (); }

在这种情况下，安装第二个表格会破坏第一个表格。

这些模式很好地指示了我们的组件在哪里脆弱。 显示或隐藏树不应破坏该树之外的组件。

无论您是否计划渲染此组件两次，从长远来看，解决这些问题都会带来回报。 它会引导您进行更具弹性的设计。

原则 4：保持本地状态隔离

考虑一个社交媒体Post组件。 它有一个Comment线程列表（可以展开）和一个NewComment输入。

但是什么状态是真正的本地状态？ 帖子内容本身是否是本地状态？ 评论列表呢？ 或者记录哪些评论线程被扩展了？ 还是评论输入的价值？

如果您习惯于将所有内容都放入“状态管理器”中，那么回答这个问题可能具有挑战性。 所以这里有一个简单的方法来决定。

如果您不确定某些状态是否是本地的，请问自己：“如果这个组件被渲染了两次，这个交互是否应该反映在另一个副本中？” 每当答案为“否”时，您就会找到一些本地状态。

例如，假设我们渲染了同一个Post两次。 让我们看看它内部可以改变的不同事物。

  • 发布内容。 我们希望在一棵树中编辑帖子以在另一棵树中更新它。 因此，它可能不应该是Post组件的本地状态。 评论列表。 这类似于帖子内容。 我们希望在一棵树中添加新评论，以反映在另一棵树中。 所以理想情况下我们会为它使用某种缓存，它不应该是我们Post的本地状态。

  • 扩展了哪些评论。 如果在一棵树中扩展注释也会在另一棵树中扩展它，那就很奇怪了。 在这种情况下，我们正在与特定的Comment UI 表示交互，而不是抽象的“评论实体”。 因此，“扩展”标志应该是Comment的本地状态。

  • 新评论输入的值。 如果在一个输入中键入注释也会更新另一棵树中的输入，那将很奇怪。 除非输入明确地组合在一起，否则通常人们希望它们是独立的。 所以输入值应该是NewComment组件的本地状态。

我不建议对这些规则进行教条式的解释。 当然，在更简单的应用程序中，您可能希望对所有内容使用本地状态，包括那些“缓存”。 我只是从第一原则谈理想的用户体验。

避免使真正的本地状态成为全球状态。 这与我们的“弹性”主题有关：组件之间发生的令人惊讶的同步更少。 作为奖励，这还解决了一大类性能问题。 当您的状态处于正确的位置时，“过度渲染”就不再是一个问题。

回顾

让我们再回顾一下这些原则：

  1. 不要停止数据流Props 和 state 可以改变，组件应该在这些改变发生时处理它们。
  2. 随时准备渲染组件不应该因为渲染的频率或多或少而中断。
  3. 没有组件是单例的。 即使一个组件只渲染一次，如果渲染两次不破坏它，你的设计也会改进。
  4. 保持本地状态隔离考虑哪个状态是特定 UI 表示的本地状态 — 不要将该状态提升到不必要的高度。

这些原则可帮助您编写针对更改**优化的组件。 添加、更改和删除它们很容易。**

最重要的是，一旦我们的组件具有弹性，我们就可以回到是否应该按字母排序道具的紧迫困境。



原文:

When people start learning React, they often ask for a style guide. While it’s a good idea to have some consistent rules applied across a project, a lot of them are arbitrary — and so React doesn’t have a strong opinion about them.

You can use different type of systems, prefer function declarations or arrow functions, sort your props in alphabetical order or in an order you find pleasing.

This flexibility allows integrating React into projects with existing conventions. But it also invites endless debates.

*There are important design principles that every component should strive to follow. But I don’t think style guides capture those principles well. *

Don’t Get Distracted by Imaginary Problems

Before we talk about component design principles, I want to say a few words about style guides. This isn’t a popular opinion but someone needs to say it!

In the JavaScript community, there are a few strict opinionated style guides enforced by a linter. My personal observation is that they tend to create more friction than they’re worth. I can’t count how many times somebody showed me some absolutely valid code and said “React complains about this”, but it was their lint config complaining! This leads to three issues:

  • People get used to seeing the linter as an overzealous noisy gatekeeper rather than a helpful tool. Useful warnings are drowned out by a sea of style nits. As a result, people don’t scan the linter messages while debugging, and miss helpful tips. Additionally, people who are less used to writing JavaScript (for example, designers) have a harder time working with the code.

  • People don’t learn to differentiate between valid and invalid uses of a certain pattern. For example, there is a popular rule that forbids calling setState inside componentDidMount. But if it was always “bad”, React simply wouldn’t allow it! There is a legitimate use case for it, and that is to measure the DOM node layout — e.g. to position a tooltip. I’ve seen people “work around” this rule by adding a setTimeout which completely misses the point.

  • Eventually, people adopt the “enforcer mindset” and get opinionated about things that don’t bring a meaningful difference but are easy to scan for in the code. “You used a function declaration, but our project uses arrow functions.” Whenever I have a strong feeling about enforcing a rule like this, looking deeper reveals that I invested emotional effort into this rule — and struggle to let it go. It lulls me into a false sense of accomplishment without improving my code.

Am I saying that we should stop linting? Not at all!

With a good config, a linter is a great tool to catch bugs before they happen. It’s focusing on the style too much that turns it into a distraction.

Your Lint Config

Here’s what I suggest you to do on Monday. Gather your team for half an hour, go through every lint rule enabled in your project’s config, and ask yourself: “Has this rule ever helped us catch a bug?” If not, turn it off. (You can also start from a clean slate with eslint-config-react-app which has no styling rules.)

At the very least, your team should have a process for removing rules that cause friction. Don’t assume that whatever you or something somebody else added to your lint config a year ago is a “best practice”. Question it and look for answers. Don’t let anyone tell you you’re not smart enough to pick your lint rules.

But what about formatting? Use Prettier and forget about the “style nits”. You don’t need a tool to shout at you for putting an extra space if another tool can fix it for you. Use the linter to find bugs, not enforcing the a e s t h e t i c s.

Of course, there are aspects of the coding style that aren’t directly related to formatting but can still be annoying when inconsistent across the project.

However, many of them are too subtle to catch with a lint rule anyway. This is why it’s important to build trust between the team members, and to share useful learnings in the form of a wiki page or a short design guide.

Not everything is worth automating! The insights gained from actually reading the rationale in such a guide can be more valuable than following the “rules”.

But if following a strict style guide is a distraction, what’s actually important?

That’s the topic of this post.

No amount of indentation or sorting imports alphabetically can fix a broken design. So instead of focusing on how some code looks, I will focus on how it works. There’s a few component design principles that I find very helpful:

  1. Don’t stop the data flow
  2. Always be ready to render
  3. No component is a singleton
  4. Keep the local state isolated

Even if you don’t use React, you’ll likely discover the same principles by trial and error for any UI component model with the unidirectional data flow.

Principle 1: Don’t Stop the Data Flow

Don’t Stop the Data Flow in Rendering

When somebody uses your component, they expect that they can pass different props to it over time, and that the component will reflect those changes:

// isOk might be driven by state and can change at any time
<Button color={isOk ? 'blue' : 'red'} />

In general, this is how React works by default. If you use a color prop inside a Button component, you’ll see the value provided from above for that render:

function Button({ color, children }) { return ( // ✅ `color` is always fresh! <button className={'Button-' + color}> {children} </button> );
}

However, a common mistake when learning React is to copy props into state:

class Button extends React.Component { state = { color: this.props.color }; render() { const { color } = this.state; // 🔴 `color` is stale! return ( <button className={'Button-' + color}> {this.props.children} </button> ); }
}

This might seem more intuitive at first if you used classes outside of React. However, by copying a prop into state you’re ignoring all updates to it.

// 🔴 No longer works for updates with the above implementation
<Button color={isOk ? 'blue' : 'red'} />

In the rare case that this behavior is intentional, make sure to call that prop initialColor or defaultColor to clarify that changes to it are ignored.

But usually you’ll want to read the props directly in your component and avoid copying props (or anything computed from the props) into state:

function Button({ color, children }) { return ( // ✅ `color` is always fresh! <button className={'Button-' + color}> {children} </button> );
}

Computed values are another reason people sometimes attempt to copy props into state. For example, imagine that we determined the button text color based on an expensive computation with background color as an argument:

class Button extends React.Component { state = { textColor: slowlyCalculateTextColor(this.props.color) }; render() { return ( <button className={ 'Button-' + this.props.color + ' Button-text-' + this.state.textColor // 🔴 Stale on `color` prop updates }> {this.props.children} </button> ); }
}

This component is buggy because it doesn’t recalculate this.state.textColor on the color prop change. The easiest fix would be to move the textColor calculation into the render method, and make this a PureComponent:

class Button extends React.PureComponent { render() { const textColor = slowlyCalculateTextColor(this.props.color); return ( <button className={ 'Button-' + this.props.color + ' Button-text-' + textColor // ✅ Always fresh }> {this.props.children} </button> ); }
}

Problem solved! Now if props change, we'll recalculate textColor, but we avoid the expensive computation on the same props.

However, we might want to optimize it further. What if it’s the children prop that changed? It seems unfortunate to recalculate the textColor in that case. Our second attempt might be to invoke the calculation in componentDidUpdate:

class Button extends React.Component { state = { textColor: slowlyCalculateTextColor(this.props.color) }; componentDidUpdate(prevProps) { if (prevProps.color !== this.props.color) { // 😔 Extra re-render for every update this.setState({ textColor: slowlyCalculateTextColor(this.props.color), }); } } render() { return ( <button className={ 'Button-' + this.props.color + ' Button-text-' + this.state.textColor // ✅ Fresh on final render }> {this.props.children} </button> ); }
}

However, this would mean our component does a second re-render after every change. That’s not ideal either if we’re trying to optimize it.

You could use the legacy componentWillReceiveProps lifecycle for this. However, people often put side effects there too. That, in turn, often causes problems for the upcoming Concurrent Rendering features like Time Slicing and Suspense. And the “safer” getDerivedStateFromProps method is clunky.

Let’s step back for a second. Effectively, we want memoization. We have some inputs, and we don’t want to recalculate the output unless the inputs change.

With a class, you could use a helper for memoization. However, Hooks take this a step further, giving you a built-in way to memoize expensive computations:

function Button({ color, children }) { const textColor = useMemo( () => slowlyCalculateTextColor(color), [color] // ✅ Don’t recalculate until `color` changes ); return ( <button className={'Button-' + color + ' Button-text-' + textColor}> {children} </button> );
}

That’s all the code you need!

In a class component, you can use a helper like memoize-one for that. In a function component, useMemo Hook gives you similar functionality.

Now we see that even optimizing expensive computations isn’t a good reason to copy props into state. Our rendering result should respect changes to props.

Don’t Stop the Data Flow in Side Effects

So far, we’ve talked about how to keep the rendering result consistent with prop changes. Avoiding copying props into state is a part of that. However, it is important that side effects (e.g. data fetching) are also a part of the data flow.

Consider this React component:

class SearchResults extends React.Component { state = { data: null }; componentDidMount() { this.fetchResults(); } fetchResults() { const url = this.getFetchUrl(); // Do the fetching... } getFetchUrl() { return 'http://myapi/results?query' + this.props.query; } render() { // ... }
}

A lot of React components are like this — but if we look a bit closer, we'll notice a bug. The fetchResults method uses the query prop for data fetching:

  getFetchUrl() {
    return 'http://myapi/results?query' + this.props.query;
  }

But what if the query prop changes? In our component, nothing will happen. This means our component’s side effects don’t respect changes to its props. This is a very common source of bugs in React applications.

In order to fix our component, we need to:

  • Look at componentDidMount and every method called from it.
    • In our example, that’s fetchResults and getFetchUrl.
  • Write down all props and state used by those methods.
    • In our example, that’s this.props.query.
  • Make sure that whenever those props change, we re-run the side effect.
    • We can do this by adding the componentDidUpdate method. 
class SearchResults extends React.Component { state = { data: null }; componentDidMount() { this.fetchResults(); } componentDidUpdate(prevProps) { if (prevProps.query !== this.props.query) { // ✅ Refetch on change this.fetchResults(); } } fetchResults() { const url = this.getFetchUrl(); // Do the fetching... } getFetchUrl() { return 'http://myapi/results?query' + this.props.query; // ✅ Updates are handled } render() { // ... }
}

Now our code respects all changes to props, even for side effects.

However, it’s challenging to remember not to break it again. For example, we might add currentPage to the local state, and use it in getFetchUrl:

class SearchResults extends React.Component { state = { data: null, currentPage: 0, }; componentDidMount() { this.fetchResults(); } componentDidUpdate(prevProps) { if (prevProps.query !== this.props.query) { this.fetchResults(); } } fetchResults() { const url = this.getFetchUrl(); // Do the fetching... } getFetchUrl() { return ( 'http://myapi/results?query' + this.props.query + '&page=' + this.state.currentPage // 🔴 Updates are ignored ); } render() { // ... }
}

Alas, our code is again buggy because our side effect doesn’t respect changes to currentPage.

Props and state are a part of the React data flow. Both rendering and side effects should reflect changes in that data flow, not ignore them!

To fix our code, we can repeat the steps above:

  • Look at componentDidMount and every method called from it.
    • In our example, that’s fetchResults and getFetchUrl.
  • Write down all props and state used by those methods.
    • In our example, that’s this.props.query and this.state.currentPage.
  • Make sure that whenever those props change, we re-run the side effect.
    • We can do this by changing the componentDidUpdate method.

Let’s fix our component to handle updates to the currentPage state:

class SearchResults extends React.Component { state = { data: null, currentPage: 0, }; componentDidMount() { this.fetchResults(); } componentDidUpdate(prevProps, prevState) { if ( prevState.currentPage !== this.state.currentPage || // ✅ Refetch on change prevProps.query !== this.props.query ) { this.fetchResults(); } } fetchResults() { const url = this.getFetchUrl(); // Do the fetching... } getFetchUrl() { return ( 'http://myapi/results?query' + this.props.query + '&page=' + this.state.currentPage // ✅ Updates are handled ); } render() { // ... }
}

Wouldn’t it be nice if we could somehow automatically catch these mistakes? Isn’t that something a linter could help us with?

Unfortunately, automatically checking a class component for consistency is too difficult. Any method can call any other method. Statically analyzing calls from componentDidMount and componentDidUpdate is fraught with false positives.

However, one could design an API that can be statically analyzed for consistency. The React useEffect Hook is an example of such API:

function SearchResults({ query }) { const [data, setData] = useState(null); const [currentPage, setCurrentPage] = useState(0); useEffect(() => { function fetchResults() { const url = getFetchUrl(); // Do the fetching... } function getFetchUrl() { return ( 'http://myapi/results?query' + query + '&page=' + currentPage ); } fetchResults(); }, [currentPage, query]); // ✅ Refetch on change // ...
}

We put the logic inside of the effect, and that makes it easier to see which values from the React data flow it depends on. These values are called “dependencies”, and in our example they are [currentPage, query].

Note how this array of “effect dependencies” isn’t really a new concept. In a class, we had to search for these “dependencies” through all the method calls. The useEffect API just makes the same concept explicit.

This, in turn, lets us validate them automatically:

(This is a demo of the new recommended exhaustive-deps lint rule which is a part of eslint-plugin-react-hooks. It will soon be included in Create React App.)

Note that it is important to respect all prop and state updates for effects regardless of whether you’re writing component as a class or a function.

With the class API, you have to think about consistency yourself, and verify that changes to every relevant prop or state are handled by componentDidUpdate. Otherwise, your component is not resilient to prop and state changes. This is not even a React-specific problem. It applies to any UI library that lets you handle “creation” and “updates” separately.

The useEffect API flips the default by encouraging consistency. This might feel unfamiliar at first, but as a result your component becomes more resilient to changes in the logic. And since the “dependencies” are now explicit, we can verify the effect is consistent using a lint rule. We’re using a linter to catch bugs!

Don’t Stop the Data Flow in Optimizations

There's one more case where you might accidentally ignore changes to props. This mistake can occur when you’re manually optimizing your components.

Note that optimization approaches that use shallow equality like PureComponent and React.memo with the default comparison are safe.

However, if you try to “optimize” a component by writing your own comparison, you may mistakenly forget to compare function props:

class Button extends React.Component { shouldComponentUpdate(prevProps) { // 🔴 Doesn't compare this.props.onClick  return this.props.color !== prevProps.color; } render() { const onClick = this.props.onClick; // 🔴 Doesn't reflect updates const textColor = slowlyCalculateTextColor(this.props.color); return ( <button onClick={onClick} className={'Button-' + this.props.color + ' Button-text-' + textColor}> {this.props.children} </button> ); }
}

It is easy to miss this mistake at first because with classes, you’d usually pass a method down, and so it would have the same identity anyway:

class MyForm extends React.Component { handleClick = () => { // ✅ Always the same function // Do something } render() { return ( <> <h1>Hello!</h1> <Button color='green' onClick={this.handleClick}> Press me </Button> </> ) }
}

So our optimization doesn’t break immediately. However, it will keep “seeing” the old onClick value if it changes over time but other props don’t:

class MyForm extends React.Component { state = { isEnabled: true }; handleClick = () => { this.setState({ isEnabled: false }); // Do something } render() { return ( <> <h1>Hello!</h1> <Button color='green' onClick={ // 🔴 Button ignores updates to the onClick prop this.state.isEnabled ? this.handleClick : null }> Press me </Button> </> ) }
}

In this example, clicking the button should disable it — but this doesn’t happen because the Button component ignores any updates to the onClick prop.

This could get even more confusing if the function identity itself depends on something that could change over time, like draft.content in this example:

 drafts.map(draft => <Button color='blue' key={draft.id} onClick={ // 🔴 Button ignores updates to the onClick prop this.handlePublish.bind(this, draft.content) }> Publish </Button> )

While draft.content could change over time, our Button component ignored change to the onClick prop so it continues to see the “first version” of the onClick bound method with the original draft.content.

So how do we avoid this problem?

I recommend to avoid manually implementing shouldComponentUpdate and to avoid specifying a custom comparison to React.memo(). The default shallow comparison in React.memo will respect changing function identity:

function Button({ onClick, color, children }) { const textColor = slowlyCalculateTextColor(color); return ( <button onClick={onClick} className={'Button-' + color + ' Button-text-' + textColor}> {children} </button> );
}
export default React.memo(Button); // ✅ Uses shallow comparison

In a class, PureComponent has the same behavior.

This ensures that passing a different function as a prop will always work.

If you insist on a custom comparison, make sure that you don’t skip functions:

 shouldComponentUpdate(prevProps) { // ✅ Compares this.props.onClick  return ( this.props.color !== prevProps.color || this.props.onClick !== prevProps.onClick ); }

As I mentioned earlier, it’s easy to miss this problem in a class component because method identities are often stable (but not always — and that’s where the bugs become difficult to debug). With Hooks, the situation is a bit different:

To sum up this section, don’t stop the data flow!

Whenever you use props and state, consider what should happen if they change. In most cases, a component shouldn’t treat the initial render and updates differently. That makes it resilient to changes in the logic.

With classes, it’s easy to forget about updates when using props and state inside the lifecycle methods. Hooks nudge you to do the right thing — but it takes some mental adjustment if you’re not used to already doing it.

Principle 2: Always Be Ready to Render

React components let you write rendering code without worrying too much about time. You describe how the UI should look at any given moment, and React makes it happen. Take advantage of that model!

Don’t try to introduce unnecessary timing assumptions into your component behavior. Your component should be ready to re-render at any time.

How can one violate this principle?

React doesn’t make it very easy — but you can do it by using the legacy componentWillReceiveProps lifecycle method:

class TextInput extends React.Component { state = { value: '' }; // 🔴 Resets local state on every parent render componentWillReceiveProps(nextProps) { this.setState({ value: nextProps.value }); } handleChange = (e) => { this.setState({ value: e.target.value }); }; render() { return ( <input value={this.state.value} onChange={this.handleChange} /> ); }
}

In this example, we keep value in the local state, but we also receive value from props. Whenever we “receive new props”, we reset the value in state.

The problem with this pattern is that it entirely relies on accidental timing.

Maybe today this component’s parent updates rarely, and so our TextInput only “receives props” when something important happens, like saving a form.

But tomorrow you might add some animation to the parent of TextInput. If its parent re-renders more often, it will keep “blowing away” the child state! You can read more about this problem in “You Probably Don’t Need Derived State”.

So how can we fix this?

First of all, we need to fix our mental model. We need to stop thinking of “receiving props” as something different from just “rendering”. A re-render caused by a parent shouldn’t behave differently from a re-render caused by our own local state change. Components should be resilient to rendering less or more often because otherwise they’re too coupled to their particular parents.

(This demo shows how re-rendering can break fragile components.)

While there are a few different solutions for when you truly want to derive state from props, usually you should use either a fully controlled component:

// Option 1: Fully controlled component.
function TextInput({ value, onChange }) { return ( <input value={value} onChange={onChange} /> );
}

Or you can use an uncontrolled component with a key to reset it:

// Option 2: Fully uncontrolled component.
function TextInput() { const [value, setValue] = useState(''); return ( <input value={value} onChange={e => setValue(e.target.value)} /> );
} // We can reset its internal state later by changing the key:
<TextInput key={formId} />

The takeaway from this section is that your component shouldn’t break just because it or its parent re-renders more often. The React API design makes it easy if you avoid the legacy componentWillReceiveProps lifecycle method.

To stress-test your component, you can temporarily add this code to its parent:

componentDidMount() { // Don't forget to remove this immediately! setInterval(() => this.forceUpdate(), 100);
}

Don’t leave this code in — it’s just a quick way to check what happens when a parent re-renders more often than you expected. It shouldn’t break the child!

You might be thinking: “I’ll keep resetting state when the props change, but will prevent unnecessary re-renders with PureComponent”.

This code should work, right?

// 🤔 Should prevent unnecessary re-renders... right?
class TextInput extends React.PureComponent {
  state = {
    value: ''
  };
  // 🔴 Resets local state on every parent render
  componentWillReceiveProps(nextProps) {
    this.setState({ value: nextProps.value });
  }
  handleChange = (e) => {
    this.setState({ value: e.target.value });
  };
  render() {
    return (
      <input
        value={this.state.value}
        onChange={this.handleChange}
      />
    );
  }
}

At first, it might seem like this component solves the problem of “blowing away” the state on parent re-render. After all, if the props are the same, we just skip the update — and so componentWillReceiveProps doesn’t get called.

However, this gives us a false sense of security. This component is still not resilient to actual prop changes. For example, if we added another often-changing prop, like an animated style, we would still “lose” the internal state:

<TextInput style={{opacity: someValueFromState}} value={ // 🔴 componentWillReceiveProps in TextInput // resets to this value on every animation tick. value }
/>

So this approach is still flawed. We can see that various optimizations like PureComponent, shouldComponentUpdate, and React.memo shouldn’t be used for controlling behavior. Only use them to improve performance where it helps. If removing an optimization breaks a component, it was too fragile to begin with.

The solution here is the same as we described earlier. Don’t treat “receiving props” as a special event. Avoid “syncing” props and state. In most cases, every value should either be fully controlled (through props), or fully uncontrolled (in local state). Avoid derived state when you can. And always be ready to render!

Principle 3: No Component Is a Singleton

Sometimes we assume a certain component is only ever displayed once. Such as a navigation bar. This might be true for some time. However, this assumption often causes design problems that only surface much later.

For example, maybe you need to implement an animation between two Page components on a route change — the previous Page and the next Page. Both of them need to be mounted during the animation. However, you might discover that each of those components assumes it’s the only Page on the screen.

It’s easy to check for these problems. Just for fun, try to render your app twice:

ReactDOM.render( <> <MyApp /> <MyApp /> </>, document.getElementById('root')
);

Click around. (You might need to tweak some CSS for this experiment.)

Does your app still behave as expected? Or do you see strange crashes and errors? It’s a good idea to do this stress test on complex components once in a while, and ensure that multiple copies of them don’t conflict with one another.

An example of a problematic pattern I’ve written myself a few times is performing global state “cleanup” in componentWillUnmount:

componentWillUnmount() {
  // Resets something in Redux store
  this.props.resetForm();
}

Of course, if there are two such components on the page, unmounting one of them can break the other one. Resetting “global” state on mount is no better:

componentDidMount() { // Resets something in Redux store this.props.resetForm();
}

In that case mounting a second form will break the first one.

These patterns are good indicators of where our components are fragile. Showing or hiding a tree shouldn’t break components outside of that tree.

Whether you plan to render this component twice or not, solving these issues pays off in the longer term. It leads you to a more resilient design.

Principle 4: Keep the Local State Isolated

Consider a social media Post component. It has a list of Comment threads (that can be expanded) and a NewComment input.

React components may have local state. But what state is truly local? Is the post content itself local state or not? What about the list of comments? Or the record of which comment threads are expanded? Or the value of the comment input?

If you’re used to putting everything into a “state manager”, answering this question can be challenging. So here’s a simple way to decide.

If you’re not sure whether some state is local, ask yourself: “If this component was rendered twice, should this interaction reflect in the other copy?” Whenever the answer is “no”, you found some local state.

For example, imagine we rendered the same Post twice. Let’s look at different things inside of it that can change.

  • Post content. We’d want editing the post in one tree to update it in another tree. Therefore, it probably should not be the local state of a Post component. (Instead, the post content could live in some cache like Apollo, Relay, or Redux.)

  • List of comments. This is similar to post content. We’d want adding a new comment in one tree to be reflected in the other tree too. So ideally we would use some kind of a cache for it, and it should not be a local state of our Post.

  • Which comments are expanded. It would be weird if expanding a comment in one tree would also expand it in another tree. In this case we’re interacting with a particular Comment UI representation rather than an abstract “comment entity”. Therefore, an “expanded” flag should be a local state of the Comment.

  • The value of new comment input. It would be odd if typing a comment in one input would also update an input in another tree. Unless inputs are clearly grouped together, usually people expect them to be independent. So the input value should be a local state of the NewComment component.

I don’t suggest a dogmatic interpretation of these rules. Of course, in a simpler app you might want to use local state for everything, including those “caches”. I’m only talking about the ideal user experience from the first principles.

Avoid making truly local state global. This plays into our topic of “resilience”: there’s fewer surprising synchronization happening between components. As a bonus, this also fixes a large class of performance issues. “Over-rendering” is much less of an issue when your state is in the right place.

Recap

Let’s recap these principles one more time:

  1. Don’t stop the data flow Props and state can change, and components should handle those changes whenever they happen.
  2. Always be ready to render A component shouldn’t break because it’s rendered more or less often.
  3. No component is a singleton. Even if a component is rendered just once, your design will improve if rendering twice doesn’t break it.
  4. Keep the local state isolated Think about which state is local to a particular UI representation — and don’t hoist that state higher than necessary.

These principles help you write components that are **optimized for change . It’s easy to add, change them, and delete them.**

And most importantly, once our components are resilient, we can come back to the pressing dilemma of whether or not props should be sorted by alphabet.

item.title: 随着决赛临近，加州大学罢工进入第三周，紧张局势加剧 | UC strike enters third week as finals near, tensions mount
item.description:
阿瓦伊斯·费萨尔

发表于随着全国有史以来最大规模的高等教育学术工作者罢工进入第三周星期一，距离期末考试的关键时间只有几天，人们越来越担心加州大学教学和研究的核心任务会产生长期和意想不到的后果.

教师们尤其担心，如果没有更多的州或联邦资金来支付 48,000 名罢工工人的工资需求，劳动力成本会增加，这可能会迫使减少招聘研究生——危及他们进行的研究和学术经验。他们帮助教的本科生。 如果加州大学的资助申请价格更高，它们的竞争力可能会降低，这可能会影响该大学在气候变化、基因工程、经济不平等和银河之谜等领域的变革性工作，仅举几例。

教师导师和研究生之间最重要的关系正在受到考验，一些派系之间的怨恨正在恶化。 由加州大学所有 10 个校区和劳伦斯伯克利国家实验室的四个美国汽车工人联合会谈判小组发起的集体劳工行动，也引发了长期存在的抱怨，即国家对学生的资助不足以及加州大学在“行政膨胀”上的支出不当，作为一名教员成员说，过度支持学术。

评分中断迫在眉睫，一些校园的课程最早在周五结束，而期末考试将在接下来的一两周开始。 在上周发给全系统教师的一封信中，学术参议院领导人建议，如果罢工继续，他们可以雇用临时读者或将期末考试设为可选，前提是这样做不会产生有害后果，例如剥夺学生提高成绩的机会. 教师还可以利用一些校区宣布的延长截止日期来提交最终成绩。

一旦暂停，usamaasif2334 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

原文:

awais faisal

Posted on Dec 4

As the nation’s largest ever strike of higher-education academic workers enters its third week Monday, with the crunch time of final exams just days away, fears are rising over long-lasting and unintended consequences to the University of California’s core missions of teaching and research.

Faculty in particular are worried that higher labor costs to meet the salary demands of the 48,000 striking workers, without more state or federal funding to pay for them, could force cutbacks in hiring graduate students — jeopardizing the research they conduct and the academic experiences of the undergraduates they help teach. UC grant applications could become less competitive if they have higher price tags, potentially affecting the university’s transformative work in climate change, genetic engineering, economic inequality and galactic mysteries, to name a few areas.

The all-important relationship between faculty mentors and graduate students is being tested, with bitterness festering among some factions. The collective labor action, by four United Auto Workers bargaining units across all 10 UC campuses and the Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, is also surfacing long-standing complaints about inadequate state funding for students and misplaced UC spending on “administrative bloat,” as one faculty member put it, over support for academics.

Disruptions over grading are looming, with classes ending as early as Friday on some campuses, and finals starting in the next week or two. In a letter sent to systemwide faculty last week, Academic Senate leaders advised that if the strike continues, they could hire temporary readers or make final exams optional if doing so would not have harmful consequences, such as depriving students of a chance to raise their grades. Faculty can also take advantage of extended deadlines, which some campuses have announced, to submit final grades.

Read More

item.title: 官员称，联邦快递司机绑架了周五被发现死亡的 7 岁德州女孩 | FedEx driver kidnapped 7-year-old Texas girl who was found dead Friday, officials say
item.description:
阿瓦伊斯·费萨尔

发表于官员称，德克萨斯州一名联邦快递合同司机周五被捕，并被指控绑架和谋杀一名两天前失踪的 7 岁女孩。

雅典娜·斯特兰德 (Athena Strand) 于周三从她位于怀斯县天堂 (Paradise) 的家中消失，她的失踪促使社区约 200 名志愿者展开大规模搜索。

雅典娜斯特兰德。 周五，当局在约 11 英里外的博伊德市东南部发现了她的尸体，怀斯县治安官莱恩·阿金告诉记者。

阿金说，司机是 31 岁的坦纳·林恩·霍纳 (Tanner Lynn Horner)，他已因谋杀罪和严重绑架罪被捕。

周五晚上，他被关押在监狱中，以代替 150 万美元的保释金。 周五晚上还不清楚霍纳是否有可以代表他发言的律师。

阿金周五晚上说，霍纳在被发现失踪的前后在家里送了一个包裹。 他说，调查人员认为这名女孩在绑架后大约一个小时内死亡。

雅罗斯拉夫波利亚科夫 - 一旦暂停，usamaasif2334 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

原文:

awais faisal

Posted on Dec 4

A FedEx contract driver in Texas was arrested Friday and accused of kidnapping and murder in the death of a 7-year-old girl who went missing two days earlier, officials said.

The disappearance of Athena Strand, who vanished Wednesday from her home in Paradise in Wise County, prompted a large search with around 200 volunteers from the community.

Athena Strand. (KXAS)

On Friday, authorities found her body southeast of the city of Boyd, a city around 11 miles away, Wise County Sheriff Lane Akin told reporters.

“We do have a confession,” Akin said at a news conference Friday night.

The driver, Tanner Lynn Horner, 31, has been arrested on charges of capital murder and aggravated kidnapping, Akin said.

He was being held in jail in lieu of $1.5 million bond Friday night. It was not immediately clear Friday night if Horner had an attorney who could speak on his behalf.

Athena arrived home from school Wednesday afternoon like she normally does and around 6:40 p.m. her stepmother reported the child missing, Akin has said.

Horner delivered a package at the home around the time she was discovered missing, Akin said Friday night. Investigators believe the girl died within about an hour of the abduction, he said.

Jishnu Prasad Samal - Dec 3

Yaroslav Polyakov - Dec 3

item.title: 在没有 ifconfig 或 ip 命令的情况下在 Ubuntu 中获取 ip 地址 | Get the ip address in Ubuntu without ifconfig or ip command
item.description:
如何使用 Linux

发表于问题：
今天我得到了一个新的 Ubuntu 服务器。 我试图用 ifconfig 或 ip 命令获取它的 IP 地址。

但是我收到一个命令未找到错误。 我在谷歌上搜索了错误，看起来我需要安装包才能运行这些命令。

但是我没有 sudo 访问权限。 如果没有这些命令，还有其他方法可以获取 IP 地址吗？

回答 1 ：您可以尝试使用 Python。 它默认安装在大多数发行版上。 这是非常容易使用。 

 $python >>> import netifaces as ni >>> ni.interfaces() ['lo', 'eth0','eth1','eth2'] >>> ni.ifaddresses('eth0') {17: [{'addr': '02:93:07:be:da:d9', 'broadcast': 'ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff'}], 2: [{'addr': '10.0.0.76', 'netmask': '255.255.255.0', 'broadcast': '10.0.0.255'}], 10: [{'addr': 'fe80::93:7ff:febe:dad9%eth0', 'netmask': 'ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff::'}]}

在上面的例子中，有四个接口lo、eth0、eth1、eth2。 一台 Linux 服务器可以有多个网络接口。 您可以在此处查看我在我的网站上发布的帖子，以获取更多相关信息。

此 Python 脚本显示每个网络接口的 mac 地址、ip 地址和网络掩码。

这是另一种不使用 python 包获取 IP 地址的方法：

 import socket import fcntl import struct def get_ip_address(ifname): s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_DGRAM) return socket.inet_ntoa(fcntl.ioctl( s.fileno(), 0x8915, # SIOCGIFADDR struct.pack('256s', ifname[:15]) )[20:24]) get_ip_address('eth0') # '192.168.0.110'

你可以参考这篇文章来了解更多关于如何使用 Python 获取 ip 地址的信息。

回答2

如果您只是 Ubuntu 服务器上的用户，而管理员已禁止您访问某些工具，则可能很难确定您可以使用哪些工具。 或者，您可以尝试使用curl访问将检测您的公共 IP 的外部服务。 那里有很多 - 我只是找到了我找到的第一个搜索结果：

德文帕特里克阿德金斯 - 一旦暂停，howtuselinux 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 howtouselinux 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

howtouselinux

Posted on Dec 4

Question:
Today I got a new Ubuntu server. I tried to get its IP address with the ifconfig or ip commands.

But I got a command not found error. I searched the errors on Google, and it looks like I need to install package to run these commands.

But I don't have sudo access. Is there any other way to get the IP address without these commands?

Answer1: You can try using Python. It is installed by default on most distributions. It is very easy to use.

$python
>>> import netifaces as ni
>>> ni.interfaces()
['lo', 'eth0','eth1','eth2']
>>> ni.ifaddresses('eth0')
{17: [{'addr': '02:93:07:be:da:d9', 'broadcast': 'ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff'}], 2: [{'addr': '10.0.0.76', 'netmask': '255.255.255.0', 'broadcast': '10.0.0.255'}], 10: [{'addr': 'fe80::93:7ff:febe:dad9%eth0', 'netmask': 'ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff::'}]}

In the above example, there are four interfaces lo, eth0, eth1, eth2. The ip address for eth0 is 10.0.0.76.

A Linux server can have more than one network interface. You can check out my post on my website here to get more info about this.

This Python script displays the mac address, ip address, netmask for each network interface.

Here's another way to get the IP address without using a python package:

import socket
import fcntl
import struct

def get_ip_address(ifname):
    s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_DGRAM)
    return socket.inet_ntoa(fcntl.ioctl(
        s.fileno(),
        0x8915,  # SIOCGIFADDR
        struct.pack('256s', ifname[:15])
    )[20:24])

get_ip_address('eth0')  # '192.168.0.110'

You can refer to this post to get more about how to use Python to get ip address.

Answer2

If you are simply a user on an Ubuntu server, and the administrator has disabled your access to certain tools, it may be difficult to determine which tools you have available. The ip command is typically available to end-users, but perhaps there's a path issue (unlikely):

/usr/bin/ip addr show
# or
/bin/ip addr show
# or (could be restricted)
/sbin/ip addr show

Alternatively, you can try using curl to access an external service that will detect your public IP. There are quite a few out there - I just went with the first search result I found:

DevonPatrick Adkins - Nov 19

item.title: 德州7岁女孩尸体被发现，联邦快递司机被捕 | Body of 7-year-old Texas girl found, FedEx driver arrested
item.description:
阿瓦伊斯·费萨尔

发表于德州7岁女孩尸体被发现，联邦快递司机被捕

德克萨斯州天堂市（美联社）——当局称，一名 7 岁的德克萨斯州女孩在被报失踪两天后被发现死亡，一名联邦快递送货司机因她的死亡被捕。

据怀斯县警长莱恩·阿金 (Wise County Sheriff Lane Akin) 称，雅典娜·斯特兰德 (Athena Strand) 的尸体于周五被发现，31 岁的坦纳·林恩·霍纳 (Tanner Lynn Horner) 在承认杀害了这名女孩并告诉当局在哪里可以找到她的尸体后，以绑架和谋杀罪名被捕。

周六，霍纳仍以 150 万美元的保释金入狱。 监狱记录没有列出可以代表他发言的律师。

阿金在周五晚些时候的新闻发布会上说，当局找到了霍纳的线索，警长说霍纳在女孩失踪前不久向她家送货。

据阿金说，霍纳不认识女孩的家人，他拒绝讨论犯罪动机。

原文:

awais faisal

Posted on Dec 4

Body of 7-year-old Texas girl found, FedEx driver arrested

PARADISE, Texas (AP) — A 7-year-old Texas girl has been found dead, two days after being reported missing, and a FedEx delivery driver arrested in her death, authorities said.

The body of Athena Strand was found Friday and Tanner Lynn Horner, 31, was arrested on kidnapping and murder charges after confessing to killing the girl and telling authorities where to find her body, according to Wise County Sheriff Lane Akin.

Horner remained jailed Saturday on $1.5 million bond. Jail records did not list an attorney who could speak on his behalf.

Akin said during a late Friday news conference that a tip led authorities to Horner, who the sheriff said had made a delivery to the girl’s home shortly before she disappeared.

Horner did not know the girl’s family, according to Akin, who declined to discuss a motive for the crime.

“We really can’t get into the content of the confession, but I will say we have a confession” from Horner, Akin said.

item.title: Nextjs中生成sitemap.xml和robots.txt | Generate sitemap.xml and robots.txt in Nextjs
item.description:
博扬·贾格蒂奇

发表于当您创建您的网站时，您希望它在谷歌搜索结果中排在第一位，或者换句话说，我们需要改进我们的搜索引擎优化 (SEO)。 谷歌根据许多不同的原因对网站进行排名，但最重要的原因之一是它了解我们的网站并知道对它的期望。 这就是为什么我们

需要sitemap.xml和robots.txt 。

网站地图

让我们从站点地图代表什么以及它如何工作开始。 站点地图是一个文件，您可以在其中提供有关站点上的页面、视频和其他文件以及它们之间关系的信息。 站点地图告诉 Google 您认为哪些页面和文件在您的站点中很重要，并且还提供有关这些文件的有价值的信息。 搜索引擎利用此文件更准确地索引您的网站。 您可以添加额外的

比如上次更新是什么时候，页面变化的频率，优先级等等。

静态站点地图

当您拥有静态网站时，静态站点地图将完成这项工作。换句话说，当您的网站不经常更改时，您可以制作简单的 .xml 文件来定义和告诉谷歌爬虫您拥有哪些内容。

 <urlset xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9"> <url> <loc>https://yourapp.com</loc> </url> <url> <loc>https://yourapp.com/blog</loc> </url> <url> <loc>https://yourapp.com/libary</loc> </url> <url> <loc>https://yourapp.com/contact</loc> </url> </urlset>

动态站点地图

另一方面，如果您的站点经常更改，则需要制作动态站点地图。 您可以通过在获取所有文件后生成 .xml 文件来手动执行此操作，但在本文中，我们将介绍更简单的方法。
有一个名为next-sitemap的很棒的 npm 模块，它正在为你做所有脏活。

首先，您需要使用以下命令安装它：

创建站点地图配置文件以供下一个站点地图使用。 有许多可用的属性，但我们将使用这三个：

  • 默认为假
  • 如果 URL 数量超过默认值，它将创建新的 sitemap.xml，因此您将拥有 sitemap-0.xml 和 sitemap-1.xml 等。 默认值为 5000。
 module . exports = { siteUrl : process . env . SITE_URL || ' https://yourapp.com ' , generateRobotsTxt : true , // (optional) sitemapSize : 7000 }

在您的package.json添加 postbuild 脚本，该脚本将在成功构建后自动触发，我们将在其中启动next-sitemap命令。

 { "build" : "next build" , "postbuild" : "next-sitemap"
}

输出

构建完成后，您将默认在public中生成 sitemap.xml 和 sitemap-0.xml。 如果将 generateRobotsTxt 设置为 true，您也会获得 robots.txt 文件。

如果检查 sitemap.xml 你应该看到：

 <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <sitemapindex xmlns= "http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9" > <sitemap><loc> https://bojanjagetic.com/sitemap-0.xml </loc></sitemap> </sitemapindex>

如您所见，只有一个位置引用了我们的sitemap-0.xml 。 让我们打开并检查它的内容：

 <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <urlset xmlns= "http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9" xmlns:news= "http://www.google.com/schemas/sitemap-news/0.9" xmlns:xhtml= "http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" xmlns:mobile= "http://www.google.com/schemas/sitemap-mobile/1.0" xmlns:image= "http://www.google.com/schemas/sitemap-image/1.1" xmlns:video= "http://www.google.com/schemas/sitemap-video/1.1" > <url><loc> https://bojanjagetic.com </loc><lastmod> 2022-12-03T20:01:39.202Z </lastmod><changefreq> daily </changefreq><priority> 0.7 </priority></url> <url><loc> https://bojanjagetic.com/routes/aboutme </loc><lastmod> 2022-12-03T20:01:39.203Z </lastmod><changefreq> daily </changefreq><priority> 0.7 </priority></url> <url><loc> https://bojanjagetic.com/routes/blog </loc><lastmod> 2022-12-03T20:01:39.203Z </lastmod><changefreq> daily </changefreq><priority> 0.7 </priority></url> <url><loc> https://bojanjagetic.com/post/npm-vs-yarn </loc><lastmod> 2022-12-03T20:01:39.203Z </lastmod><changefreq> daily </changefreq><priority> 0.7 </priority></url> <url><loc> https://bojanjagetic.com/post/programming-concepts </loc><lastmod> 2022-12-03T20:01:39.203Z </lastmod><changefreq> daily </changefreq><priority> 0.7 </priority></url> <url><loc> https://bojanjagetic.com/libary/crypto-scrapper </loc><lastmod> 2022-12-03T20:01:39.203Z </lastmod><changefreq> daily </changefreq><priority> 0.7 </priority></url> <url><loc> https://bojanjagetic.com/libary/github-card-npm-component </loc><lastmod> 2022-12-03T20:01:39.203Z </lastmod><changefreq> daily </changefreq><priority> 0.7 </priority></url> ...

因此，正如您所见，它生成了我拥有的所有路线，以便 Google 爬虫知道哪些资源可用。

机器人

正如我们已经提到的， robots.txt将告诉 Google 爬虫可以请求哪些文件和资源以及站点地图的位置。 生成的 robots.txt 内容如下：

 # * User-agent: * Allow: / # Host Host: https://bojanjagetic.com # Sitemaps Sitemap: https://bojanjagetic.com/sitemap.xml

结论

知道我们有sitemap.xml和robots.txt我们可以知道在 Google 搜索中获得更好的可见性并且排名会更好，这意味着我们将获得更多访问者。

克里斯托弗·凯德—— 一旦暂停，basskibo 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，basskibo 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，basskibo 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 basskibo 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 basskibo：

报告其他不当行为

Unflagging basskibo will restore default visibility to their posts. Unflagning basskibo 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Bojan Jagetic

Posted on Dec 4

When you make your site you want it to be first in google search results or in other words we need to make improvements to our Search Engine Optimization (SEO). Google rank websites by many different reasons but one of the most important is that it knows our site and know what to expect on it. That is the reason why we

need sitemap.xml and robots.txt.

Robot.txt tells Google crawler which files it can request from website and which cannot.

Sitemap

Lets begin with what sitemap represent and how it works. A sitemap is a file where you provide information about the pages, videos, and other files on your site, and the relationships between them. Search engines like Google read this file to crawl your site more efficiently. A sitemap tells Google which pages and files you think are important in your site, and also provides valuable information about these files. What sitemap.xml do is that is basically defining relationship between pages on website. Search engines utilize this file to more accurately index your site. You can add additional

things like when was the last time it was updated, how frequently the pages changes, priority, etc.

text='Sitemap flow' alt='Sitemap flow'

/>

Static Sitemap

When you have static website, static sitemap will do the job.In other words when your website does not change frequently you can make simple .xml file for defining and telling google crawler which content you have.

<urlset xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9">
  <url>
      <loc>https://yourapp.com</loc>
  </url>
  <url>
      <loc>https://yourapp.com/blog</loc>
  </url>
  <url>
      <loc>https://yourapp.com/libary</loc>
  </url>
  <url>
      <loc>https://yourapp.com/contact</loc>
  </url>
</urlset>

Dynamic Sitemap

On the other hand, if your site frequently changes, you need to make dynamic sitemap. You can do it manually by generating .xml file after fetching all your files, but in this post we will cover the easier way of doing so.
There is great npm module called next-sitemap which is doing all dirty work for you.

First you need to install it by using following command:

Create site map configuration file for next-sitemap to use. There are many properties available but we will use these three:

  • siteUrl- used for setting base URL of your website
  • generateRobotsTxt- Generate a robots.txt file and list the generated sitemaps. Default false
  • sitemapSize- Split large sitemap into multiple files by specifying sitemap size. If number of URLs reach over default it will create new sitemap.xml so you will have sitemap-0.xml and sitemap-1.xml,etc. Default is 5000.
module.exports = { siteUrl: process.env.SITE_URL || 'https://yourapp.com', generateRobotsTxt: true, // (optional) sitemapSize: 7000
}

In your package.json add postbuild script which will be automatically triggered after succesfull build where we will start next-sitemap command.

{ "build": "next build", "postbuild": "next-sitemap"
}

Output

After build is done you will have generated sitemap.xml and sitemap-0.xml at public folder by default. If you set generateRobotsTxt to true you will get robots.txt file as well.

If check sitemap.xml you should see:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<sitemapindex xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9">
<sitemap><loc>https://bojanjagetic.com/sitemap-0.xml</loc></sitemap>
</sitemapindex>

As you can see there is only one location referencing to our sitemap-0.xml. Lets open and check content of it:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<urlset xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9" xmlns:news="http://www.google.com/schemas/sitemap-news/0.9" xmlns:xhtml="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" xmlns:mobile="http://www.google.com/schemas/sitemap-mobile/1.0" xmlns:image="http://www.google.com/schemas/sitemap-image/1.1" xmlns:video="http://www.google.com/schemas/sitemap-video/1.1">
<url><loc>https://bojanjagetic.com</loc><lastmod>2022-12-03T20:01:39.202Z</lastmod><changefreq>daily</changefreq><priority>0.7</priority></url>
<url><loc>https://bojanjagetic.com/routes/aboutme</loc><lastmod>2022-12-03T20:01:39.203Z</lastmod><changefreq>daily</changefreq><priority>0.7</priority></url>
<url><loc>https://bojanjagetic.com/routes/blog</loc><lastmod>2022-12-03T20:01:39.203Z</lastmod><changefreq>daily</changefreq><priority>0.7</priority></url>
<url><loc>https://bojanjagetic.com/post/npm-vs-yarn</loc><lastmod>2022-12-03T20:01:39.203Z</lastmod><changefreq>daily</changefreq><priority>0.7</priority></url>
<url><loc>https://bojanjagetic.com/post/programming-concepts</loc><lastmod>2022-12-03T20:01:39.203Z</lastmod><changefreq>daily</changefreq><priority>0.7</priority></url>
<url><loc>https://bojanjagetic.com/libary/crypto-scrapper</loc><lastmod>2022-12-03T20:01:39.203Z</lastmod><changefreq>daily</changefreq><priority>0.7</priority></url>
<url><loc>https://bojanjagetic.com/libary/github-card-npm-component</loc><lastmod>2022-12-03T20:01:39.203Z</lastmod><changefreq>daily</changefreq><priority>0.7</priority></url>
...

So as you can see it generated all routes that I have so Google crawler knows which resources are available.

Robots

As we mention already, robots.txt will tell Google crawler which files and resources can be requested and location of sitemap. Content of generated robots.txt is something like following:

# *
User-agent: *
Allow: /

# Host
Host: https://bojanjagetic.com

# Sitemaps
Sitemap: https://bojanjagetic.com/sitemap.xml

Conclusion

Know that we have sitemap.xml and robots.txt we can know get better visibility on Google search and it will be better ranked, which means we will get more visitors.

Christopher Kade - Nov 30

Odewole Babatunde Samson - Nov 29

item.title: 如何在 Linux 中更改帐户密码到期日期 | how to change the account password expiration date in Linux
item.description:
发表于问题：
我对 Linux 中的密码到期日期感到困惑。 看起来 chage 命令 -e 更改的是帐户到期日期而不是密码到期日期。

两件事是完全不同的。 我找不到任何可用于更改密码到期日期的命令。

所有 Linux 命令都会更改用户到期日期。 有什么办法吗？ 非常感谢。

回答：

使用 chage -M。 例如，要将 90 天设置为密码到期前的最长天数，请输入 sudo chage -M 90 。

编辑：要设置确切的到期日期，您需要计算出上次密码更改日期（请参阅 chage -l）和您想要的密码到期日期之间的天数，然后在 chage -M 中使用该数字。

参考： https ://www.howtuselinux.com/post/change-user-password-expiration-date-in-linux

一旦暂停，hemanlinux 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

一旦解除暂停，hemanlinux 将可以再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，hemanlinux 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 hemanlinux 没有暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 hemanlinux 的操作：

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 hemanlinux 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

hemanlinux

Posted on Dec 4

Question:
I am confused about the password expiration date in Linux. Looks like chage command -e changes the account expiration date not the password expiration date.

Two things are totally different. I can not find any command that can be used to change the password expiration date.

All Linux commands change the user expiration date. is there any way to do this? Thanks a lot.

Answer:

Use chage -M. For example, to set 90 days as the maximum before password expiry enter sudo chage -M 90 .

EDIT: To set an exact expiration date you need to figure out how many days that are between the Last password change date (see chage -l) and your desired password expiration date and then use that number in chage -M.

Reference: https://www.howtouselinux.com/post/change-user-password-expiration-date-in-linux

item.title: 刚刚在我的 Mac 和 iPhone 上用 Orion 替换了 Safari | Just replaced Safari with Orion on my Mac and my iPhone
item.description:
是的，几周来我一直在演示Orion作为Safari的可能替代品（不失去 Safari 的许多功能），不久前 Orion 成为我的 Mac 和 iPhone 的日常驱动程序。

请注意，这不是我的日常开发驱动程序，我最近为此切换到Vivaldi 。

我一直喜欢 Orion 的几件事。

这是野生动物园

在引擎盖下它基本上运行 Webkit，当然在 iOS 上它显然运行相同。 这意味着我不会放弃在图像/视频中选择文本等功能。

紧凑的工具栏

我喜欢普通的选项卡，我不希望我的 UI 占用大量空间。 我也不确定我将如何使用此功能（单独的配置文件，想想 Firefox 容器），但它可能会在以后派上用场......

选项卡组/管理窗口

我喜欢 Safari 的一件事是选项卡组功能。 阅读清单

我也没有放弃 Safari 的阅读列表功能。

您甚至可以管理您的阅读列表，这是对 Safari 的巨大改进！

内置广告拦截

我必须下载一个扩展程序才能在 Safari 中屏蔽广告。 扩展

我通常不会在 Safari 中使用扩展来随意浏览（广告拦截除外）。 但我已经确认您可以安装 Chrome 和 Firefox 扩展程序。

现在您可以在 iOS 中设置默认浏览器，我非常喜欢 iOS 应用程序的一些功能。

有时我想看看我的家人不小心看到的网站，尤其是当我的孩子拿着我的手机听 Taylor swift 的时候。 您不仅可以拥有私人标签，还可以将它们放在指纹后面。 好的。

我知道你在想什么……停下来。

丰富的设置

扩展

我也没有玩过这个，但是能够在 iOS 中使用扩展很好。

在我的键盘上使用这些小符号可以让导航变得更加容易。 我一直在等待它出现在 Safari 中......

密码仍然有效

这只是勉强触及表面，但这是一个艰难的决定。 我尽可能多地尝试使用 Apple 生态系统应用程序。 但是，Orion 给了我在不丢失 Safari 的情况下替换 Safari 的机会，所以我做出了转换。

明天我可能会因为某种原因直接回到 Safari，我不知道。 但是 Orion（目前，在 Safari 改进之前）似乎是一个不错的选择。



原文:

Yup, I’ve been demoing Orion as a possible replacement for Safari (w/out loosing many of the features of Safari) for weeks now, and moments ago Orion became my daily driver for both my Mac and my iPhone.

Note, it is not my daily-driver for development, I recently switched to Vivaldi for that.

A few things I have been loving about Orion.

It's Safari

Under the hood it's basically running Webkit, and of course on iOS it's obviously running the same. This means I don't loose out on features like selecting text in images/video, etc.

Compact toolbar

I like normal tabs and I don't want my UI taking up a ton of space. Orion features a truly compact toolbar and nice rounded tabs.

I'm also not sure what I will do with this feature (separate profiles, think Firefox containers), but it might come in handy later...

Tab Groups / Manage Windows

One thing I love about Safari is the Tab Group features. Orion does the same thing called Manage Windows, and it even opens a new window for the session vs. switching away from your active session.

Reading List

I didn't loose out on the Reading List feature of Safari either.

You can even manage your reading list, which is a huge improvement over Safari!

Ad blocking built-in

I had to download an extension to get ad-blocking into Safari. Orion comes with it built-in!

Extensions

I don't usually use extensions much in Safari for casual browsing (except for ad-blocking). But I have confirmed that you can install Chrome & Firefox extensions.

iOS

Now that you can set a default browser in iOS, there are a few features I really like about the iOS app.

Touch-ID protected private mode

Sometimes I want to look at web-sites w/out my family accidentally seeing it, especially with my kids grabbing my phone to listen to Taylor swift. Not only can you have private tabs, but you can also put them behind your finger-print. Nice.

I know what you're thinking....stop.

Rich Settings

The settings in the iOS app have just about everything, down to the user-agent used, etc.

Extensions

I haven't played around with this either, but being able to use extensions in iOS is nice.

.com and @ in my keyboard

Having these little symbols in my keyboard makes navigating things a lot easier. I've been waiting for this to show up in Safari...

Passwords still work

This just barely touches the surface, but it was a hard decision. I try and use the Apple eco-system apps as much as I can. But, Orion gave me an opporitunity to replace Safari withouth losing Safari, so I made the switch.

Tomorrow I might go right back to Safari for some reason, I don't know. But Orion (for now, until Safari improves) seems like a solid choice.
item.author: aubreypwd

item.title: 作业跟踪器项目 | Homework Tracker Project
item.description:
介绍

这个博客基于我的第一个使用 Javascript、HTML 和 CSS 的单页应用程序。 我的想法是基于我现在的职业，即教育。 我决定创建一个应用程序，您可以在其中记录家庭作业。 我的一些学生很难保持井井有条，无法跟上他们必须完成的作业和截止日期。 我希望这可以成为一个应用程序的开始，该应用程序可以帮助我的学生跟踪家庭作业并使用它来满足截止日期并为未来创建新的作业。 随着技术的进步，我希望将更多的技术融入我的学生，以便他们有更多的接触机会，并对这些应用程序的工作原理产生探究性思考。

数据库.JSON

我的数据库由一个名为“classAssignments”的对象数组组成，其中所有对象都有一个 ID 号。 有几个描述对象的键。 这里有四个键：classSubject、title、dueDate 和 instructions。 标题键包含家庭作业标题的信息。 说明键告诉我们有关家庭作业的重要说明（如果适用）。 数据库中有 8 个对象可以开始，当您浏览博客时，我们可以看到可以创建和添加对象，甚至可以从数据库中删除对象。

 { "classAssignments": [ { "id": 1, "classSubject": "Science", "title": "Precipitation Note Catcher", "dueDate": "12/01/2022", "instructions": "Turn in on google classroom" }, { "id": 2, "classSubject": "Art", "title": "Baroque Art Reasearch Activity", "dueDate": "12/01/2022", "instructions": "Cite 3 sources" }, { "id": 3, "classSubject": "ELA", "title": "Identify Theme", "dueDate": "12/03/2022", "instructions": "Turn in on google classroom" }

该项目的 HTML 遵循与任何其他 HTML 一样的标准结构。 在此 HTML 中，CSS 文件链接到“head”部分，javascript 文件链接到“body”部分。 在 HTML 的“正文”中有一个“div”，其中包含项目“作业跟踪器”的标题。 下一个“div”有一个“容器”类，并保存家庭作业输入的表单，以及一个创建卡片（家庭作业）的“创建作业”按钮。 最后是最后一个“div”，它是一个容器，当通过“GET”请求获取作业分配卡片并将其放入 DOM 时，它将包含作业分配的卡片。

 <body> <div id="tracker-header" class = 'center'> <h1>Homework Tracker</h1> </div> <hr> <div class="container" style="display: block;"> <form class = "add-hw-tasks"> <h4>Create your own Homework Reminder!</h4> <input type="text" name="classSubject" value="" placeholder="Enter subject" class="input-text" /> <br /> <input type="text" name="title" value="" placeholder="Enter title" class="input-text" /> <br /> <input type="text" name="dueDate" value="" placeholder="Enter due date" class="input-text" /> <br /> <input type="text" name="instructions" value="" placeholder="Enter instructions" class="input-text" /> <br /> <input type="submit" name="submit" value="Create Assignment" class="submit" /> </form> </div> <div id="task-collection"></div> <script type="text/javascript" src="javascripts/index.js"></script> </body>

脚本文件

我首先创建了一个名为“showAssignment”的函数，该函数为家庭作业创建卡片，并通过采用匿名函数的“点击”事件侦听器删除卡片。 我首先创建了一个名为div的“div”HTML 元素来保存卡片的信息。 卡片信息由以下变量创建的 HTML 元素组成：

  • 主题（课程主题）（li）

  • 题目（作业题目）(li)

  • 日期（作业截止日期）（li）

  • 按钮（删除分配）（按钮）

我们还为这些变量添加了文本上下文，并将其对应的值添加到 db.json 文件中的对象。 然后我们创建了一个“点击”事件侦听器，该侦听器接受“删除”请求以从 db.json 文件中删除卡片。 然后，我们将构成作业分配卡信息的变量附加到“div”变量。 创建名为“assignmentCollection”的最终变量以访问 HTML 文件中的“task-collection”id。 我们将包含卡片 HTML 元素的 div 变量附加到变量“assignmentCollection”。 

 function showAssignment(assignment){ const assignmentCollection = document.getElementById("task-collection") const div = document.createElement("div") div.classList.add("card") const header = document.createElement("h3") header.textContent = "Homework Assignment" const subject = document.createElement("li") subject.textContent = `Subject: ${assignment.classSubject}` const titles = document.createElement("li") titles.textContent = `Title: ${assignment.title}` const date = document.createElement("li") date.textContent = `Date due: ${assignment.dueDate}` const instruction = document.createElement("li") instruction.textContent = `Instructions: ${assignment.instructions}` const button = document.createElement("button") button.classList.add("delete-btn") button.textContent = "Delete Assignment" button.id = assignment.id button.addEventListener('click', () => { fetch(`http://localhost:3000/classAssignments/${assignment.id}`, { method: 'DELETE' }) .then(() => div.remove()) }) div.append(header, subject, titles, date, instruction, button) assignmentCollection.append(div) }

我的下一个函数“getAssignment”是一个显示从 db.json 文件检索数据的“GET”请求的函数。 它为 db.json 文件运行的服务器获取 url。 它使用 forEach 迭代方法迭代数据，该方法调用 db.json 文件的每个对象。 最后，我们通过“showAssignment”函数来显示带有信息的家庭作业卡片以及创建的“删除作业”按钮。 最后我们调用获取请求的函数。

 function getAssignments(){ fetch("http://localhost:3000/classAssignments") .then(response => response.json()) .then(data => data.forEach(assignment => showAssignment(assignment))) }

在我通知您我的下一个函数之前，我将首先创建一个名为 form 的全局变量，它选择 HTML 文件中表单的“add-hw-tasks”类。 我的下一个功能称为“addAssignment”，它为家庭作业创建卡片。 我们首先向作为此函数的参数传递的事件添加“preventDefault”。 这样表单就不会自行提交。 然后我们创建一个数组变量，以事件中的相应值为目标。 由于我们想通过表单中的“提交”按钮添加卡片，因此我们向运行 db.json 文件的服务器创建的 url 调用“POST”请求。 正文是 JSON.stringify，包含卡片的相应值：课程主题、作业标题、截止日期和相应家庭作业的说明。 然后我们将 reset 方法添加到表单变量中，这样当我们提交卡信息时，信息字段是空白的，并准备添加具有新信息的不同卡。 最后，我们有一个“提交”类型的表单变量的事件侦听器，并将“addAssignment”作为唯一的回调函数传递。

 const form = document.querySelector(".add-hw-tasks") form.addEventListener('submit', addAssignment) function addAssignment(event){ event.preventDefault() const [classSubject, title, dueDate, instructions] = event.target fetch("http://localhost:3000/classAssignments", { method: "POST", headers: { "content-type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify({ classSubject: classSubject.value, title: title.value, dueDate: dueDate.value, instructions: instructions.value }) }) .then(response => response.json()) .then(response => showAssignment(response)) form.reset() }

当您的光标悬停在该 javascript 文件的最后一部分上时，它会突出显示黄色的“创建分配”按钮。 我为英语学习者、特殊教育学生和所有其他不同学习者的学生创建了这个视觉表示。 我首先创建了一个名为“buttonChange”的全局变量，它选择了表单中“Create Assignment”按钮的“submit”类。 我创建了两个名为“mouseEnter”和“mouseLeave”的函数。 函数“mouseEnter”采用“buttonChange”并将背景样式设置为黄色。 函数“mouseLeave”采用“buttonChange”并且没有设置背景样式。 最后，我们有两个事件侦听器调用了“buttonChange”变量，其中类型为“mouseenter”和“mouseleave”。 这些函数有各自的回调函数“mouseEnter”和“mouseLeave”。

 const buttonChange = document.querySelector(".submit") function mouseEnter(){ buttonChange.style.background = 'yellow' } function mouseLeave(){ buttonChange.style.background = '' } buttonChange.addEventListener("mouseenter", mouseEnter) buttonChange.addEventListener("mouseleave", mouseLeave)

将来我想为交互性和动态交互添加更多功能和事件监听器。 感谢您花时间看我的项目。

英迪拉·库马尔 AK- 一旦暂停，hluis91 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。
